test_plan.strategy: Unit tests for all logic/persistence modules; integration tests for file I/O, git execution, and bundled-SPA assertions; E2E tests via Node/HTTP clients for the admin API and via Playwright for the browser dashboard. Every FR must have at minimum a happy-path, edge-case, and negative test. All Given/When/Then use concrete values per SPEC-SEALED rule.

test_cases.title: Admin API — POST /api/projects with type='local' + valid dir returns 201 and persists entry
preconditions: ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json does not exist or is empty
test_cases.given: server is running, projects.json is empty, /tmp/proj-a/ exists
test_cases.when: POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/projects with JSON body { type: 'local', name: 'proj-a', location: '/tmp/proj-a' }
test_cases.then: response status is 201, response body includes the new project with a generated id, projects.json now contains exactly 1 entry whose location is /tmp/proj-a
steps: Send POST /api/projects with valid local-path payload
test_cases.expected_result: projects.json persists the new local entry; admin API acknowledges with 201

test_cases.title: Admin API — POST /api/projects with type='folder' scans and persists child projects
preconditions: /tmp/scan-root/ contains 2 child dirs each with package.json, and 1 child with neither package.json nor .aitri/
test_cases.given: /tmp/scan-root/ has children: childA/package.json, childB/package.json, childC/README.md
test_cases.when: POST /api/projects with JSON body { type: 'folder', location: '/tmp/scan-root' }
test_cases.then: response status is 201, projects.json contains 2 new entries for childA and childB, childC is skipped
steps: Assert projects.json contains exactly 2 new entries
Assert the skipped child (childC) is not in the list
test_cases.expected_result: Folder scan persists only children that look like projects (package.json or .aitri/)

test_cases.title: Admin API — non-loopback peer calling /api/projects is rejected with 403
test_cases.given: server is listening on loopback only
test_cases.when: a request arrives at /api/projects with a non-loopback remote address (simulated via X-Forwarded-For or by binding test client to a non-loopback interface)
test_cases.then: response status is 403 and projects.json is unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: Admin API refuses non-loopback peers with 403; no side effects on persisted state

test_cases.title: aitri-reader — valid .aitri with approvedPhases extracts all fields
test_cases.given: /tmp/aitri-proj-1/.aitri contains { "projectName": "finance-app", "currentPhase": 4, "approvedPhases": [1,2,3], "completedPhases": [1,2,3,4], "verifyPassed": true, "verifySummary": { "passed": 28, "failed": 0, "skipped": 2, "total": 30 }, "artifactHashes": { "1": "abc123" }, "rejections": {} }
test_cases.then: returned object has: currentPhase=4, approvedPhases=[1,2,3], completedPhases=[1,2,3,4], verifyPassed=true, verifySummary.passed=28, verifySummary.failed=0, hasDrift=false, lastRejection=null
test_cases.expected_result: All fields extracted correctly; hasDrift=false because no artifact hash mismatch

test_cases.title: aitri-reader — .aitri with rejection entry extracts most recent rejection
test_cases.given: /tmp/aitri-proj-2/.aitri contains { "currentPhase": 2, "approvedPhases": [1], "completedPhases": [1,2], "rejections": { "2": { "at": "2026-03-10T12:00:00Z", "feedback": "Missing API docs" } } }
test_cases.then: returned object has lastRejection = { phase: 2, at: '2026-03-10T12:00:00Z', feedback: 'Missing API docs' }
steps: Write .aitri with rejections to temp dir
test_cases.expected_result: lastRejection extracted with correct phase, timestamp, and feedback string

test_cases.title: aitri-reader — malformed .aitri JSON returns null without throwing
test_cases.given: /tmp/aitri-proj-3/.aitri contains the string '{ "currentPhase": 2, INVALID JSON'
test_cases.then: function returns null, no exception is thrown, no process crash occurs
test_cases.expected_result: Returns null; does not throw; calling code sets project status to 'unreadable'

test_cases.title: git-reader — active git repo returns correct lastCommitAt and branch
preconditions: /tmp/git-proj-1/ is an initialized git repository with at least 1 commit on branch 'main'
Most recent commit was made at 2026-03-13T08:00:00Z (2.5 hours before test time)
test_cases.given: a git repository at /tmp/git-proj-1/ with HEAD at branch 'main' and last commit ISO timestamp '2026-03-13T08:00:00Z' (2.5h ago relative to test run at 10:30:00Z)
test_cases.then: returned object has: isGitRepo=true, branch='main', lastCommitAt='2026-03-13T08:00:00Z', lastCommitAgeHours between 2.4 and 2.6, commitVelocity7d >= 1
steps: Initialize git repo in temp dir, set committer date explicitly to 2026-03-13T08:00:00Z
test_cases.expected_result: isGitRepo=true, branch='main', lastCommitAt accurate to within 1 minute of actual commit time

test_cases.title: git-reader — commit exactly 73 hours ago triggers stale alert via evaluateAlerts
preconditions: readGitMeta returns lastCommitAgeHours=73.0 for the test project
test_cases.given: projectData with gitMeta.lastCommitAgeHours=73.0, all other alert conditions false
test_cases.then: returned array contains exactly 1 alert with type='stale' and message='No commits in 73h'
test_cases.expected_result: One alert { type: 'stale', message: 'No commits in 73h', severity: 'warning' } returned

test_cases.title: git-reader — directory without .git returns null without throwing
preconditions: /tmp/no-git-dir/ exists but is not a git repository (no .git subdirectory)
test_cases.given: directory /tmp/no-git-dir/ exists but has no .git folder and git rev-parse returns exit code 128
test_cases.then: function returns null, no exception thrown, no process crash
steps: Create /tmp/no-git-dir/ as plain directory (no git init)
test_cases.expected_result: Returns null; calling code shows 'Last commit: N/A' in dashboard

test_cases.title: test-reader — valid 04_TEST_RESULTS.json returns correct summary
test_cases.given: /tmp/test-proj-1/spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json contains { "summary": { "passed": 28, "failed": 0, "skipped": 2, "total": 30 }, "fr_coverage": [{ "fr_id": "FR-001", "status": "covered" }], "results": [] }
test_cases.then: returned object has: available=true, passed=28, failed=0, skipped=2, total=30, frCoverage[0].frId='FR-001', frCoverage[0].status='covered'
test_cases.expected_result: TestSummary with available=true, correct counts, and frCoverage array extracted

test_cases.title: test-reader — failed > 0 triggers tests-failing alert via evaluateAlerts
test_cases.given: projectData with testSummary = { available: true, passed: 28, failed: 2, skipped: 0, total: 30 }, all other alert conditions false
test_cases.then: returned array contains exactly 1 alert with type='tests-failing' and message='Tests failing (2)'
test_cases.expected_result: One alert { type: 'tests-failing', message: 'Tests failing (2)', severity: 'error' } returned

test_cases.title: test-reader — absent 04_TEST_RESULTS.json returns null without crashing
preconditions: /tmp/test-proj-2/ exists but has no spec/ directory
test_cases.given: directory /tmp/test-proj-2/ exists with no spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json file
test_cases.then: function returns null, no exception is thrown
test_cases.expected_result: Returns null; calling code renders 'Tests: N/A' in dashboard

test_cases.title: Empty-state — bundled SPA contains onboarding panel with /admin CTA
preconditions: docker/web-dist/assets/*.js has been built from current web/src/
test_cases.given: a fresh production build of the SPA exists in docker/web-dist/
test_cases.when: the bundled main JS is read and searched
test_cases.then: the bundle contains the literal string 'Add your first project' and the route '/admin'
steps: Assert it includes 'Add your first project'
test_cases.expected_result: Onboarding CTA is embedded in the shipped bundle; a user loading / with no projects sees the empty state

test_cases.title: Empty-state — bundled SPA contains no references to removed CLI commands
preconditions: docker/web-dist/assets/*.js has been built from current web/src/
test_cases.given: a fresh production build of the SPA exists in docker/web-dist/
test_cases.when: the bundled main JS is read and searched for CLI-wizard tokens
test_cases.then: the bundle does NOT contain 'aitri-hub setup', 'aitri-hub monitor', or 'aitri-hub init'
steps: Assert it does NOT include 'aitri-hub setup'
Assert it does NOT include 'aitri-hub monitor'
Assert it does NOT include 'aitri-hub init'
test_cases.expected_result: No stale CLI copy leaks into the shipped web bundle

test_cases.title: Empty-state — HomeView renders onboarding panel when projects array is empty
preconditions: HomeView.jsx is the component mounted at route /
test_cases.given: HomeView receives props { projects: [], loading: false }
test_cases.then: the output contains an h2 titled 'No projects yet', a primary CTA linking to /admin with label 'Add your first project', and a disclosure 'What counts as a project?'
steps: Assert output includes the onboarding title
Assert output includes the /admin CTA label
test_cases.expected_result: Browser-first empty state replaces any CLI-wizard prompt; FR-005 AC-007/AC-008 satisfied

test_cases.title: Web dashboard — localhost:3000 returns HTTP 200 within 30s of docker compose up
test_cases.given: Docker is running, aitri-hub-web image is available, ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json contains { projects: [{ name: 'proj-a', status: 'healthy' }, { name: 'proj-b', status: 'warning' }] }
test_cases.when: docker compose up -d is run and HTTP GET http://localhost:3000 is polled every second for up to 30 seconds
test_cases.then: HTTP GET http://localhost:3000 returns status 200 with Content-Type containing 'text/html' within 30 seconds
test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 200 with text/html content type received at localhost:3000 within 30 seconds

test_cases.title: Web dashboard — 768px viewport renders all cards without horizontal scroll
test_cases.given: web dashboard running at localhost:3000 with dashboard.json containing 4 projects, Playwright browser set to viewport { width: 768, height: 1024 }
test_cases.when: page navigates to http://localhost:3000 and page load is awaited
test_cases.then: page has no horizontal scrollbar (document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= 768), all 4 project names are visible in the DOM, and no element has offsetLeft + offsetWidth > 768
steps: Launch Playwright browser with viewport { width: 768, height: 1024 }
test_cases.expected_result: scrollWidth <= 768px, 4 project cards visible, no horizontal scroll present

test_cases.title: Web dashboard — missing dashboard.json shows onboarding empty state
preconditions: ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json does NOT exist (deleted or never written)
test_cases.given: web dashboard running at localhost:3000, GET /data/dashboard.json returns HTTP 404
test_cases.when: page navigates to http://localhost:3000 and waits 3 seconds
test_cases.then: page contains the onboarding title 'No projects yet' and the CTA 'Add your first project' linking to /admin; zero project cards are rendered (data-testid='project-card' count === 0)
steps: Ensure dashboard.json does not exist in mounted volume
Assert page contains 'No projects yet' text
Assert page contains 'Add your first project' text
test_cases.expected_result: Onboarding empty state with /admin CTA shown; no project cards rendered; no JavaScript errors in console

test_cases.title: Alerts engine — healthy project with all conditions OK returns empty alerts array
test_cases.given: projectData = { aitriState: { verifyPassed: true, verifySummary: { failed: 0 }, hasDrift: false }, gitMeta: { lastCommitAgeHours: 1.5 }, testSummary: { available: true, failed: 0 } }
test_cases.then: returned array has length === 0
steps: Construct projectData with all healthy values
test_cases.expected_result: Empty array [] returned; no alerts generated for a fully healthy project

test_cases.title: Alerts engine — project with 3 concurrent alert conditions returns 3 alerts
test_cases.given: projectData = { aitriState: { verifyPassed: false, verifySummary: { failed: 2 }, hasDrift: true }, gitMeta: { lastCommitAgeHours: 90.0 }, testSummary: { available: true, failed: 3 } }
test_cases.then: returned array has length === 4 and contains alerts with types: 'stale', 'verify-failed', 'drift', 'tests-failing'
steps: Construct projectData with all 4 alert conditions active
test_cases.expected_result: 4 alerts returned (stale, verify-failed, drift, tests-failing); all with correct type and severity fields

test_cases.title: Alerts engine — verifyPassed=false generates verify-failed alert with severity=error
test_cases.given: projectData = { aitriState: { verifyPassed: false, verifySummary: { failed: 1 }, hasDrift: false }, gitMeta: { lastCommitAgeHours: 1.0 }, testSummary: null }
test_cases.then: returned array contains exactly 1 alert with type='verify-failed', severity='error', and message='Verify failed'
test_cases.expected_result: One alert { type: 'verify-failed', severity: 'error', message: 'Verify failed' }

test_cases.title: git-reader — first monitor run clones remote URL to cache directory
preconditions: ~/.aitri-hub/cache/ directory does not contain a 'test-remote-repo' subdirectory
A bare git repo exists at /tmp/fake-remote-repo.git (local git server for testing)
test_cases.given: projectEntry = { type: 'remote', location: 'file:///tmp/fake-remote-repo.git', name: 'fake-remote-repo' } and ~/.aitri-hub/cache/fake-remote-repo/ does not exist
test_cases.then: ~/.aitri-hub/cache/fake-remote-repo/ directory exists and contains a .git subdirectory; returned projectData.gitMeta.isGitRepo === true
steps: Create bare git repo at /tmp/fake-remote-repo.git with git init --bare and one commit
test_cases.expected_result: Repository cloned to ~/.aitri-hub/cache/fake-remote-repo/; gitMeta populated from cloned repo

test_cases.title: git-reader — second monitor run uses git pull on existing cache, not re-clone
preconditions: ~/.aitri-hub/cache/cached-proj/ already exists as a valid git clone
test_cases.given: ~/.aitri-hub/cache/cached-proj/.git exists (previously cloned), projectEntry.location = 'file:///tmp/fake-remote-repo.git'
test_cases.when: collectOne(projectEntry) is called with a spy on execSync tracking which git commands are run
test_cases.then: execSync is called with 'git pull' (or 'git fetch') but NOT with 'git clone'; cache directory is not re-created
steps: Pre-create cache/cached-proj/ as a git repo
Assert execSync was called with a command containing 'git pull' or 'git fetch'
Assert execSync was NOT called with a command containing 'git clone'
test_cases.expected_result: git pull executed on existing cache; git clone NOT executed; no directory recreation

test_cases.title: git-reader — network error during git pull shows cache-stale alert, no crash
preconditions: ~/.aitri-hub/cache/offline-proj/ exists as a valid git clone with stale data
test_cases.given: cache/offline-proj/.git exists, git pull throws execSync error 'fatal: unable to connect to remote repository'
test_cases.when: collectOne(projectEntry) is called with execSync stubbed to throw on git pull
test_cases.then: collectOne() does NOT throw; returned projectData has collectionError containing 'cache stale' or 'git pull failed'; alerts array contains { type: 'cache-stale' }
steps: Stub execSync to throw on 'git pull'
Assert result.collectionError is a non-null string
test_cases.expected_result: No crash; collectionError set; cache-stale alert generated; last cached .aitri data still returned

test_cases.title: dashboard store — writeDashboard writes valid JSON with correct schema to ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json
test_cases.given: dashboardData = { schemaVersion: '1', collectedAt: '2026-03-13T10:30:00Z', projects: [{ id: 'a1b2c3d4', name: 'finance-app', status: 'healthy', aitriState: { approvedPhases: [1,2,3] }, gitMeta: { branch: 'main', lastCommitAgeHours: 2 }, testSummary: null, alerts: [] }] }
test_cases.then: ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json exists, JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('dashboard.json')) succeeds without throwing, parsed.projects.length === 1, parsed.projects[0].name === 'finance-app', parsed.schemaVersion === '1'
steps: Parse file with JSON.parse — assert no exception
test_cases.expected_result: Valid JSON file at ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json with correct schema and project data

test_cases.title: dashboard store — atomic write prevents partial file on concurrent read
test_cases.given: a previous valid dashboard.json exists at ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json with content { schemaVersion: '1', projects: [{ name: 'old-project' }] }
test_cases.when: writeDashboard() is called with new data; fs.renameSync is stubbed to verify that a temp file is used as intermediate (temp path ends in '.tmp')
test_cases.then: writeDashboard() first writes to a path ending in '.tmp', then calls fs.renameSync from temp path to final path; the original dashboard.json is never in a partially-written state
steps: Assert fs.writeFileSync was called with a path containing '.tmp'
Assert fs.renameSync was called with (tmpPath, finalPath)
test_cases.expected_result: Write goes to .tmp file first, then atomic rename to dashboard.json; no partial write visible to readers

test_cases.title: dashboard store — write failure preserves previous dashboard.json content
test_cases.given: valid dashboard.json with { projects: [{ name: 'previous-project' }] } exists; fs.renameSync is stubbed to throw ENOSPC (disk full) error
test_cases.then: dashboard.json still contains { projects: [{ name: 'previous-project' }] }; writeDashboard() does NOT throw an unhandled exception; error is logged to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log
steps: Stub fs.renameSync to throw { code: 'ENOSPC', message: 'no space left on device' }
Assert log file contains 'ENOSPC' or 'write failed'
test_cases.expected_result: Previous dashboard.json intact; error logged; no crash propagated to monitor loop
